# uapi_authorized_key (Resource)

An SSH authorized key line for a router login. There is no per-key update: changing the key replaces it.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "uapi_authorized_key" "admin" {
  key = "ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIExampleKeyBlobReplaceMe admin@workstation"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `key` (String) Full authorized_keys line: '<type> <base64> [comment]'. This is a public key, so it is not sensitive. uapi never returns the blob, so it is not recoverable on import.

### Read-Only

- `comment` (String) Optional comment (trailing text on the key line).
- `id` (String) Stable id: sha256 prefix of the public-key blob.
- `type` (String) SSH key type (e.g. ssh-ed25519, ssh-rsa, ecdsa-sha2-nistp256).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import by id (the sha256 prefix uapi assigns to the key).
# The key blob is not returned by uapi, so you must set `key` in config after importing.
terraform import uapi_authorized_key.admin 0a1b2c3d4e5f
```
